mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and yields positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports equity valuations and generates positive monthly S&P 500 returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns during confirmed supply expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where HOUST falls below the 1.1 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields an insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in over 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL inflation surpasses 2.5 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in retail sales reflect immediate consumer spending strength, which translates directly into positive short-term earnings revisions and positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS momentum remains below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows or 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% of high-growth retail sales months.
